$comment: Intended Zenodo metadata for a future deposition of the TRI-NET γ-surface verification bundle. This file is NOT a deposition. No DOI is minted by checking this file into the repo; only the legacy φ²+φ⁻²=3 anchor DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19227877 (already referenced by the project) is asserted by this commit. See docs/RELEASE_MANIFEST_TRINET_V1.md for the artifact list, hashes, and anti-claims.
title: TRI-NET γ-surface — Verification hardening bundle v1 (claims matrix, D2D conformance, Triple-Decker FSM spec, NMSE golden vectors)
description: Repository snapshot of tt-trinity-gamma at the TRI-NET verification hardening milestone. The bundle pins (a) a 20-row falsifiable numerical claims matrix; (b) a D2D conformance vector pack (5 scenarios); (c) a Triple-Decker FSM specification (RBB → FBB → CAP_BOOST → IDLE with cooldown / brownout / overcurrent fallback); (d) a golden vector set for the GF16 vs bfloat16 NMSE protocol; (e) a CI spec gate. No silicon measurement is included. Pre-silicon estimates are tagged as such throughout (R5 honesty).

creators.name: Vasilev, Dmitrii
creators.$comment: Author identity inferred from src/d2d_holo_mesh.v header (`Author: Dmitrii Vasilev <admin@t27.ai>`). ORCID intentionally not asserted by this PR — must be filled in by the depositor before submission.

related_identifiers.$comment: Legacy φ²+φ⁻²=3 anchor + TIP v1.0 protocol record, already referenced from docs/INTERCONNECT_PROTOCOL_V1.md. Pre-existing; not minted by this PR.
Placeholder. No arXiv submission has occurred for this verification bundle. Do NOT fill until the preprint actually exists.

anti_claims: No DOI is minted by the act of committing this file to the repository. A DOI exists only after a Zenodo deposition is published and Zenodo assigns one.
No silicon measurement is asserted by this bundle.
No funding source or program acceptance is claimed.
ORCID identifiers and arXiv IDs above are placeholders; they MUST be filled in by the depositor before submission.
$schema_note: Format follows the Zenodo upload metadata schema as documented at https://developers.zenodo.org/#representation. Custom keys ($comment, $schema_note, anti_claims) are ignored by Zenodo's importer and exist only to document repo-side honesty constraints.
